itineraryStops.name: 濟州郵輪碼頭接送（濟州港——北部）
itineraryStops.description:
您的司機與英語導遊將在**濟州國際旅客航站樓**（제주항 국제여객터미널，地址：濟州市臨港路191號——聯合國地點代碼 **KRCHA**）迎候您的團隊。這座北海岸郵輪港於**2015年**正式開放，專門接待國際郵輪。航站樓設有兩個泊位——**主泊位（長360公尺、水深12公尺、承載量14萬噸）**及**應急泊位（長306公尺、水深11.5公尺、承載量8.5萬噸）**——每次只能停靠**一艘郵輪**。

接送時間設定為**船隻公告通關後30分鐘**，以應對多船同時靠泊時移民入境流程的時間差異。司機攜帶中英文姓名牌、紙本行程卡（附所有停靠時間及韓文地址，供緊急搭乘計程車使用）以及營運商24小時聯絡電話。車輛停放於入境大廳旁**可容納100輛巴士的停車場**。上車前，導遊將說明當日行程安排、返船時間、天氣狀況及任何港口日程調整。

若旅客需要外幣兌換（濟州銀行）、SIM卡／Wi-Fi租借，或使用多語言旅遊諮詢中心，導遊將全程陪同——以上服務均設於航站樓內。根據**韓國文化體育觀光部**資料，**2024年共有414艘郵輪、81萬名郵輪旅客抵達**——較上一年度幾乎翻了三倍——因此多船靠泊日出現航站樓擁擠情況實屬正常。
highlights: **船隻通關後30分鐘接送**——緩衝時間可吸收移民入境延誤
司機與英語導遊持姓名牌於入境大廳迎候
**主泊位 360公尺 × 12公尺／承載量14萬噸**（同時限一艘船靠泊）
**100輛巴士停車容量**，緊鄰航站樓——快速上車
旅遊諮詢中心、濟州銀行外幣兌換、ATM、免費Wi-Fi一應俱全
提供附有韓文地址的行程卡，作為緊急搭乘計程車之備用

sources: VisitKorea / english.visitkorea.or.kr (Jeju cruise terminal facilities + 100-bus parking)
South Korea Ministry of Oceans & Fisheries (terminal opening 2015; berth specifications)
Korea Cruise Port Sales / k-cruiseinfo.com (berth depths, tonnage capacity)
Ministry of Culture, Sports & Tourism (2024 cruise volume: 810,000 pax / 414 ships)
Jeju Provincial Government (Imhang-ro address, Geonip-dong; UN/LOCODE KRCHA)

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 於濟州國際旅客航站樓入境大廳進行**小團郵輪接送（最多8至15人）**——較小的團體規模讓導遊能在搭乘**中型客車或廂型車**前，提供個人化歡迎並確認每位旅客的特殊需求（拍照優先景點、行動不便考量、飲食偏好）。停放於100輛巴士停車場的小團車輛就在航站樓門口——加上車身較輕且機動性強，若郵輪行程有所調整，**臨時修改行程也更為輕鬆**。對於特別因為**拍照彈性與從容節奏**而選擇小團產品的旅客，30分鐘的通關緩衝時間也讓導遊得以錯開接送安排——無需等候40人大巴名單中行動較慢的下船旅客。

convenience.restroom: 於集合地點（地鐵站／飯店大廳／港口航站樓）
convenience.parking: 旅客無需步行——於車輛旁接送

smartNotes.tip: 請於預定接送時間前5至10分鐘抵達；郵輪／非軍事區行程請攜帶附照片之身分證件

itineraryStops.name: 城山日出峰
itineraryStops.category: 聯合國教科文組織凝灰岩火山錐——日出峰（三重聯合國教科文組織認證）
itineraryStops.description:
**城山日出峰（성산일출봉，「日出峰」）**是一座**距今5,000年的蘇特塞型水火山凝灰岩錐**——一種罕見地貌，由水下玄武岩噴發突破海面時形成：岩漿遇海水後產生蒸汽爆炸，堆積出高達**182公尺的要塞狀火山口**邊緣，而非典型的熔岩錐。其成果正是聯合國教科文組織正式描述的**「新生蘇特塞型火山構造的傑出典範」**。火山口**寬600公尺、深90公尺**，環繞著**99塊嶙峋礁岩**，當地傳說將其比擬為石冠。

城山日出峰擁有**三重聯合國教科文組織認證**：**世界自然遺產（2007年7月2日列入）**，隸屬**「濟州火山島與熔岩隧道」**保護地；**生物圈保護區（2002年）**；以及**全球地質公園（2010年10月）**——此三重認證在韓國景點中僅與漢拿山並列。濱海基部鑿有**18座日本帝國二戰洞穴**（1945年神風特攻隊自殺艇掩體）。步道：**單程0.8公里，爬升約140公尺，寬闊石階來回60分鐘**。

**海女（해녀）表演**每日**14:00**在火山錐下方礁石灣舉行——濟州女性潛水員（2016年聯合國教科文組織非物質文化遺產）不使用水肺潛水。**門票已於2025年4月更新**：**成人 ₩5,000／青少年及兒童 ₩2,500／團體成人 ₩1,600**，7歲以下及65歲以上免費。山頂觀景台亦列入**瀛洲十景（濟州十大勝景）**之中，並長年是韓國最重要的**元旦日出朝聖地**——每年1月1日黎明前有逾5萬名遊客登頂，自1995年起更有官方日出慶典活動。

從山頂清晰可見4公里外的**牛島（우도，「牛島」）**——韓國最大的次生火山島，可從城山港搭乘**15分鐘渡輪**抵達（來回票價 ₩10,500），適合行程有餘裕的旅客另日前往。
highlights: **三重聯合國教科文組織認證**：世界遺產 2007年7月2日 ＋ 生物圈保護區 2002年 ＋ 全球地質公園 2010年10月
**距今5,000年**——蘇特塞型水火山噴發，岩漿遇海水產生蒸汽爆炸而形成
火山口**寬600公尺×深90公尺**，環繞著**99塊嶙峋礁岩**（「石冠」）
**海拔182公尺**——單程0.8公里／爬升約140公尺，石階步道來回60分鐘
基部設有**18座日本帝國二戰洞穴**——1945年神風特攻隊自殺艇掩體
**海女表演每日14:00**於海濱礁灣舉行——2016年聯合國教科文組織非物質文化遺產
**每月第一個週一公休**（不定期休館日）
timeUsed: 停車 + 購票 + 入口說明 — 約10分鐘
登頂觀景台（0.8公里／爬升140公尺）— 約30分鐘
山頂景觀 + 火山口邊緣拍照 — 約20分鐘
下山 — 約20分鐘
可選：入口處觀賞海女表演（每日1場，下午2點；惡劣天氣時可能取消）— 約10分鐘
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **城山日出峰小團深度遊** — 90分鐘行程（比巴士團的75分鐘更從容），讓導遊得以在環形步道上的三個攝影機位停留，從容講解**5,000年前單次噴發形成的凝灰岩錐**，並可帶想欣賞海上島嶼日出的旅客沿南坡木棧道替代路線下山。**三重UNESCO認定**（2007年世界遺產 + 2002年生物圈保護區 + 2010年地質公園）是當日行程最高規格的亮點——小團形式讓導遊能面對面講解**與濟州本島相連的連島沙洲**以及步道入口的海女表演（依排程），而非透過遊覽車廣播系統傳達。

itineraryStops.name: 涉地可支（東岬角）
itineraryStops.category: 火山岬角 — 安藤忠雄建築 + 方頭浦燈塔 + 韓劇拍攝聖地
itineraryStops.description:
**涉地可支（섭지코지）**是濟州島東海岸的一處狹長火山岬角——地名結合了**「섭지」（狹窄地帶）+「코지」（濟州方言，意為「岬角」）**。**2.1公里懸崖步道（約30分鐘，僅14公尺爬升）**是島上最常被拍攝的海岸步道之一，以三處文化建築景點為核心亮點。**(1) 붉은오름（紅岳）** — 位於岬角頂端的紅色火山寄生火山錐，錐頂聳立著**八角形方頭浦燈塔（방두포 등대）**，**每4秒閃爍一次**，目前仍作為沿海航行導航設施持續運作。

**(2) 협자연대（協資烽燧）** — **以火山石砌成的朝鮮時代烽火台，高4公尺、直徑9公尺**，被濟州道指定為**第23-2號特別紀念物** — 是環繞全島38座烽燧之一，曾於16至18世紀作為抵禦海盜入侵的早期預警系統。**(3) 安藤忠雄建築** — 普立茲克獎得主、日本建築師**安藤忠雄（안도 다다오）**在此設計了兩座建築：**Glass House（글라스 하우스）**餐廳與**由民美術館（유민 미술관）**，均展現了安藤標誌性的清水混凝土與光影極簡主義風格，作為鳳凰島度假區開發項目建成。

離岸處，**高10公尺的火山頸地貌선바위（선바위，「立岩」）**出現於當地**龍王幼子**的傳說之中。此地韓劇拍攝作品包括**《All In》（2003，SBS）、《我的女孩》（2005，SBS）、《銀杏樹床邊故事》、《黃山伐》及《千一夜》**。**春季油菜花**於**3至4月（黃色）**盛開漫山遍野；**夏季繡球花**緊隨其後，於**6月（藍紫色）**綻放。岬角**免費入場**；由民美術館及Glass House餐廳分開售票／預約。
highlights: **2.1公里岬角步道**（約30分鐘，僅爬升14公尺）— 部分路段適合輪椅／嬰兒車通行
**方頭浦燈塔** — 八角形，**每4秒閃爍一次**，聳立於붉은오름（紅岳）火山錐頂端
**協資烽燧（협자연대）** — 朝鮮時代烽火台，高4公尺×直徑9公尺，**第23-2號特別紀念物**
**安藤忠雄建築** — Glass House餐廳 + 由民美術館（鳳凰島）
**선바위（立岩）** — 高10公尺的離岸火山頸地貌，與龍王幼子的在地傳說相關
**韓劇拍攝地**：《All In》（2003年）、《我的女孩》（2005年）等多部作品
**油菜花季：3至4月**（黃色花海）；**繡球花：6月**（藍紫色）
**岬角免費入場**；由民美術館 + Glass House餐廳分開售票
從岬角頂端向北望去，**城山日出峰清晰可見，距離約4公里** — 是拍攝三重UNESCO全景的絕佳背景
timeUsed: 停車 + 入場 — 約5分鐘
岬角步道漫步 — 約25分鐘
燈塔 + 協子煙臺拍照 — 約10分鐘
安藤忠雄建築外觀參觀 — 約10分鐘
返回巴士 + 緩衝時間 — 約10分鐘
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **涉地可支的安藤忠雄建築**是小團體行程的亮點之一——**普立茲克獎得主的清水混凝土極簡主義**風格的玻璃屋餐廳與由民美術館，值得近距離細細品味；小團體的時間安排讓賓客得以**進入由民美術館**（約30分鐘自由參觀），並在玻璃屋點一杯咖啡（俯瞰長達1.3公里的海岸岬角），完全無需面對旅遊巴士團的排隊人潮。**朝鮮協子煙臺烽火台**（特別紀念物第23-2號，4公尺×9公尺的火山石烽煙堆）與**防頭浦燈塔**（八角形，每4秒閃爍一次）皆附有專屬導覽解說。針對**5部韓劇的取景地**（《All In》、《我的女孩》、《銀杏樹的愛情》、《武士白東守》、《千日夜》），小團體行程讓韓劇粉絲得以在導遊帶領下找到特定場景的拍攝地點——這種繞路式的深度探訪，是大型巴士團無法提供的。

itineraryStops.name: 午餐（濟州東部——當地海鮮或黑毛豬）
itineraryStops.description:
**濟州東部午餐停留**於經導遊精選的當地餐廳，位於城山／城邑／表善一帶，專為配合郵輪當日遊的行程時間而挑選。**兩種典型選項**，依團體偏好與供應情況擇一：**(1) 濟州黑毛豬BBQ**（제주 흑돼지）——濟州的代表性食材，本土黑豬於2015年被指定為**韓國天然紀念物**，豬五花切塊在桌邊炭火燒烤，搭配傳統小菜享用；或 **(2) 帶魚燜燉**（갈치조림——帶魚燉白蘿蔔，醬油辣椒醬汁）——濟州東部著名海鮮料理，食材取自城山及城邑一帶漁港的每日鮮獲。

素食選項在大多數餐廳均可提供，需提前告知（導遊約提前30分鐘向餐廳確認）。午餐時間約為**60分鐘**，符合郵輪當日遊的緊湊行程（全日行程通常為靠港至返港共8小時）。飲品：大麥茶（免費）、本地**濟州啤酒**（約₩6,000），或**漢拿山燒酒**（濟州特產蒸餾酒，約₩5,000）。輪班餐廳均接受**主要信用卡（Visa/Mastercard）及韓圜現金**；經郵輪旅遊認證的餐廳均提供雙語圖文菜單（英文／中文）。

午餐**不含**於標準郵輪旅遊報價內——典型自費金額為**每人₩15,000–25,000**（視菜色而異）；預訂時可升級為含餐方案。
highlights: 經導遊精選的濟州東部餐廳——城山／城邑／表善一帶
**濟州黑毛豬BBQ**（2015年天然紀念物）或**帶魚燜燉**
約60分鐘——符合郵輪當日遊行程時間
雙語圖文菜單（英文／中文），接受信用卡及現金付款
素食選項須提前告知
**午餐不含**——自費約每人₩15,000–25,000（可升級為含餐方案）

itineraryStops.name: 城邑民俗村
itineraryStops.category: 朝鮮時代邑城——重要民俗文化財（1423 年建立）
itineraryStops.description:
**城邑民俗村（성읍 민속마을）** 是一座**於 1423 年朝鮮世宗大王在位期間所建立的活態朝鮮時代邑城**，當時稱為旌義縣（정의현），是朝鮮時代濟州三大行政治所之一。該村作為**縣治行政中心持續運作至 1914 年**，歷時近 **500 年**，至今在原邑城（읍성）範圍內仍保存 **110 座傳統建築**，整體格局依**朝鮮縣治典型的韓國風水原則**規劃。

全村被指定為**韓國重要民俗文化財**，另有**多棟個別建築**各自獲得獨立文化財地位。村落中心矗立著**兩棵被指定為韓國天然紀念物的古樹**——**欅樹（느티나무）**與**朴樹（팽나무）**，樹齡均估計超過 **600 年**，被視為村落的精神象徵。歷史傷痕清晰可見：**1826 年的一場大火燒毀了約 80 棟建築**，**1948 年濟州四三事件期間**村落亦遭受火災破壞——兩段歷史均記錄於村內說明牌上。

**歷史上（前現代時期）戶數高峰約達 1,500 戶**——如今村內仍有約 250 戶居民，許多人仍積極傳承傳統工藝。戲劇拍攝紀錄：**《大長今（宮中珍饌）》（MBC，2003–2004 年）**，享譽國際的韓劇代表作。當地特色：**五梅酒（오메기술）**——一種以**糯米為基底的濟州傳統酒**，由**單一家族世代釀造**，可在村內的釀酒坊免費體驗釀造過程並品嚐。**免費入場**；以戶外步行為主（約 70% 戶外 / 30% 室內韓屋參觀）。
highlights: **1423 年由世宗大王創立**——行政中心延續至 **1914 年（約 500 年）**
原邑城範圍內保存 **110 座傳統建築**
**韓國重要民俗文化財**（全村及多棟建築分別指定）
村落中心**兩棵樹齡約 600 年的古樹**：欅樹＋朴樹——**天然紀念物**
依**朝鮮韓國風水（地理）原則**規劃——典型縣治格局
**《大長今》（MBC 2003–2004）**拍攝地——國際知名韓劇
**五梅酒（오메기술）**——由單一家族世代釀造的濟州傳統米酒（免費品嚐）
歷史傷痕：**1826 年火災**燒毀約 80 棟建築；**1948 年濟州四三事件**受損
**免費入場**——目前仍有約 250 戶居民居住並傳承傳統
timeUsed: 停車＋村落解說員歡迎簡介——約 10 分鐘
步行至古樹及村落中心——約 10 分鐘

五梅酒釀造坊免費品嚐——約 10 分鐘

緩衝時間——約 5 分鐘
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **城邑民俗村小團深度體驗**——75 分鐘的參觀時間（相較於巴士行程的 60 分鐘），讓旅客得以進入**兩戶私人韓屋**與仍在居住的家庭近距離互動（此安排僅適用於小團體產品——屋主可接待 8 至 15 人共聚品茗，但大型遊覽車團體人數過多，將造成干擾）。**兩棵樹齡約 600 年的天然紀念物古樹**（欅樹＋朴樹）、**石頭爺爺（하르방）石像**，以及**五梅酒品嚐**（由單一家族世代釀造的傳統米黍酒），均可獲得充裕時間細細體驗。世宗大王於 **1423 年將城邑建立為濟州行政治所**（至 1914 年，歷時約 500 年）的歷史，及**重要民俗文化財**的指定意義，皆在與家族長輩面對面交流時娓娓道來（視預約安排而定）。

itineraryStops.name: 返回濟州郵輪碼頭（濟州港——北側）
itineraryStops.description:
驅車返回位於濟州市北岸的**濟州國際旅客碼頭**（제주항 국제여객터미널）。從濟州東部（城山／城邑地區）出發，經1132號公路（沿海環島公路）及16號道路，車程約70至80分鐘，途中設有一處指定休息站。接送時間安排為在船舶**全員登船時間至少60分鐘前**抵達碼頭，符合郵輪業登船排隊的標準緩衝時間。車輛駛入**可容納100輛巴士的停車場**，旅客在碼頭入口下車，由司機／導覽員提供行李協助。

若旅客有傍晚時段的濟州國際機場班機，**100號巴士可從郵輪碼頭直達機場**——導覽員可就此選項提供建議。碼頭內設有便利商店、藥局、小吃店、提供**免稅商品**的紀念品商店、可使用海外卡的濟州銀行ATM、外幣兌換服務（一般營業時間08:00–17:00），以及SK電信T-mobile Wi-Fi租借櫃台——適合旅客在出航前進行最後採購或補充行動數據流量。

此外，亦提供前往**東門傳統市場**（濟州市歷史最悠久、規模最大的市場，約10分鐘）的**免費接駁巴士**，供全員登船前尚有多餘時間的旅客使用。

stops.name: Jeju Cruise Terminal Pickup (Jeju Port — north)
stops.description: Your driver and English-speaking guide meet your party at the **Jeju International Passenger Terminal** (제주항 국제여객터미널, address: 191 Imhang-ro, Jeju-si — UN/LOCODE **KRCHA**), the northern coast cruise port that opened in **2015** to receive international cruise vessels. The terminal has two berths — the **Main Berth (360 m, 12 m water depth, 140,000-ton capacity)** and an **Emergency Berth (306 m, 11.5 m water depth, 85,000-ton capacity)** — handling **one cruise ship at a time**. Pickup is timed for **30 minutes after your ship's posted clearance**, absorbing immigration variance during multi-ship arrivals. The driver carries an English/Korean name placard, a printed itinerary card (with all stop times and Korean-script addresses for emergency taxi use), and the operator's 24/7 contact number. Vehicle is parked in the **100-bus parking capacity lot** adjacent to the arrival hall. Before boarding, the guide reviews the day's flow, all-aboard time, weather, and any port-day adjustments. If your guests need currency exchange (Jeju Bank), a SIM/Wi-Fi rental, or use of the multilingual tourist information center, the guide accompanies — these are all on-site within the terminal. Per **South Korea's Ministry of Culture, Sports & Tourism**, **810,000 cruise tourists arrived on 414 ships in 2024** — nearly tripling the prior year — so terminal congestion is realistic on multi-ship days.
highlights: **Pickup 30 min after ship clearance** — buffer absorbs immigration delays
Driver + English-speaking guide meet at arrival hall with name placard
**Main Berth 360 m × 12 m / 140,000-ton capacity** (single concurrent ship)
**100-bus parking** capacity adjacent to terminal — fast vehicle boarding
Tourist info center, Jeju Bank currency exchange, ATMs, free Wi-Fi on-site
Itinerary card with Korean-script addresses provided as emergency-taxi backup

Korea Cruise Port Sales / k-cruiseinfo.com (berth depths, tonnage capacity)
Ministry of Culture, Sports & Tourism (2024 cruise volume: 810,000 pax / 414 ships)

stops.whyOnRoute: **Small-group cruise pickup (8–15 pax max)** at the Jeju International Passenger Terminal arrival hall — the smaller party size lets the guide deliver a personalized welcome and confirm any guest-specific requests (photo priorities, mobility considerations, dietary preferences) before boarding the **minicoach or van**. Vehicle parking in the 100-bus lot puts the small-group vehicle right at the terminal door — and the lighter vehicle weight + maneuverability translate to **easier last-minute itinerary tuning** if the cruise schedule shifts. For guests who chose the small-group product specifically for **photo flexibility and unhurried pacing**, the 30-minute clearance buffer also means the guide can stagger arrivals — no waiting on slow disembarkers from a 40-pax coach manifest.

stops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong
stops.category: UNESCO Tuff Cone — Sunrise Peak (Triple UNESCO Designation)
stops.description: **Seongsan Ilchulbong (성산일출봉, 'Sunrise Peak')** is a **5,000-year-old Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic tuff cone** — a rare landform created when an underwater basalt eruption broke the sea surface, the magma met seawater, and the resulting steam-explosive ash built a **182 m fortress-shaped crater** rim instead of a typical lava cone. The result is what UNESCO formally calls **'an exceptional example of an emergent Surtseyan-type volcanic edifice'**. The crater is **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** that local lore likens to a stone crown. Seongsan carries the **Triple UNESCO designation**: **World Natural Heritage (inscribed 2007-07-02)** as part of the **'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' property**, **Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Global Geopark (2010-10)** — a stack matched only by Hallasan itself among Korean sites. **18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** (kamikaze suicide-boat shelters, 1945) are tunneled into the seaward base. Trail: **0.8 km one-way, ~140 m elevation gain, 60 min round trip** on wide stone steps. **Haenyeo (해녀) demonstrations** run once daily at 14:00 (1 show/day) at the rocky cove below the cone — Jeju women divers (UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage 2016) descend without scuba gear. Admission updated **April 2025**: **Adult ₩5,000 / Youth+Child ₩2,500 / Group adult ₩1,600**, free under 7 and over 65. The summit deck is also part of the **Yeongjusipgyeong (Jeju 10 Scenic Wonders) canon**, and remains Korea's premier **annual New Year sunrise pilgrimage** destination — 50,000+ visitors ascend before dawn on January 1, with municipal sunrise-festival programming since 1995. **Udo Island (우도, 'Cow Island')** — Korea's largest secondary volcanic island — is clearly visible 4 km offshore from the summit, accessible by **15-min ferry from Seongsan Port** (₩10,500 round-trip) for guests with extra time on a different itinerary.
highlights: **Triple UNESCO**: World Heritage 2007-07-02 + Biosphere Reserve 2002 + Global Geopark 2010-10
**5,000 years old** — Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic eruption, magma-meets-seawater steam-explosive birth
Crater **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** (the 'stone crown')
**182 m above sea level** — 0.8 km / ~140 m gain, 60-min round trip on stone-step trail

stops.name: Seopjikoji (East Cape)
stops.category: Volcanic Cape — Tadao Ando Architecture + Bangdupo Lighthouse + Drama Filming
stops.description: **Seopjikoji (섭지코지)** is a narrow volcanic cape on Jeju's east coast — the name compounds **'Seopji' (narrow piece of land) + 'Koji' (Jeju dialect for 'cape')**. The **2.1 km cliff-edge trail (~30 min, only 14 m elevation gain)** is one of the island's most photographed coastal walks, anchored by three cultural-architectural moments. **(1) Bulgeun Oreum (붉은 오름)** — a red-tinged volcanic parasitic cone at the cape's tip, capped by the **octagonal Bangdupo Lighthouse (방두포 등대)** which **flashes every 4 seconds** and remains in operation as a coastal navigation aid. **(2) The Hyeopja Yeondae Beacon Fire Station (협자연대)** — a **4 m tall × 9 m diameter Joseon-era smoke-signal mound built of volcanic stone**, designated **Special Monument No. 23-2** by Jeju Province — one of 38 such yeondae beacons that ringed the island as a 16th–18th-century early-warning system against pirate raids. **(3) Tadao Ando architecture** — Pulitzer-laureate Japanese architect **Tadao Ando (안도 다다오)** designed two buildings on site: the **Glass House (글라스 하우스)** restaurant and the **Yumin Art Gallery (유민 미술관)**, both characteristic of Ando's signature concrete-and-light minimalism, completed for the Phoenix Island resort development. Offshore, the **10 m volcanic neck Seonbawi (선바위, 'Seon Rock')** is featured in local **dragon king's youngest son** legend. Drama-filming credits include **'All In' (2003, SBS), 'My Girl' (2005, SBS), 'Legend of Gingko', 'The Uprising', and 'Cheonilya'**. **Spring canola fields** flood the slopes **March–April (yellow)**; **summer hydrangea blooms** follow in **June (blue/violet)**. **FREE admission** to the cape; Yumin Art Gallery and Glass House restaurant separately ticketed/reserved.

stops.name: Seongeup Folk Village
stops.category: Joseon-era Walled Town — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage (1423 founding)
stops.description: **Seongeup Folk Village (성읍 민속마을)** is a **living Joseon-era walled town founded in 1423** during the reign of **Sejong the Great** as Jeong-eui-hyeon (정의현), one of the three administrative seats of Joseon-era Jeju. The village served continuously as a **county administrative center until 1914** — nearly **500 years** — and retains **110 traditional structures** today within the original eupseong (읍성) walled-town footprint, designed on **Korean feng shui principles** typical of Joseon county capitals. The entire village is designated **Important Folklore Cultural Heritage of South Korea**, with **multiple individual buildings** carrying separate heritage status. At the village center stand **two ancient trees designated Natural Monuments of South Korea** — a **Saw-leaf Zelkova (느티나무)** and a **Japanese Hackberry (팽나무)**, both estimated **600+ years old** and revered as the village's spiritual anchor. Historical scars are visible: a **major fire in 1826 destroyed ~80 buildings**, and the village suffered fire damage **during the Jeju Uprising in 1948** — both events preserved as historical record on village signage. The **peak household count (historical, pre-modern) was ~1,500** — today the village remains inhabited by ~250 families, many actively maintaining traditional crafts. Drama filming credit: **'Dae Jang Geum (Jewel in the Palace)' (MBC, 2003–2004)**, the international K-drama hit. Local specialty: **Omegisul (오메기술)** — a **rice-based traditional Jeju liquor** brewed by **one family for multiple generations**, with the brewing demonstrated and tasted free of charge at the home-brewery within the village. **FREE admission**; outdoor walking dominates (~70% outdoor / 30% indoor for individual hanok visits).

stops.name: Gangjeong Cruise Terminal Pickup (Seogwipo — south)
stops.description: Your driver and English-speaking guide meet your party at the **Gangjeong Port Cruise Terminal** (강정 크루즈항 / 서귀포 크루즈터미널 — UN/LOCODE **KRSPO**), officially the **'Jeju Civilian-Military Complex Port for Beautiful Tourism'** (제주 민군복합형관광미항), located in Gangjeong Village on Seogwipo's southern coast. The terminal opened in **February 2016**; cruise passenger operations began **2017**. Unlike the smaller northern Jeju Port, Gangjeong has **TWO cruise berths** — the **Western Seawall (Berths 11–12, 420 m, 12–15 m depth, 220,000-ton capacity)** and the **Southern Seawall (Berths 21–22, 690 m, 17 m depth, 220,000-ton capacity)** — handling **two large cruise ships simultaneously**. Pickup is timed for **30 minutes after your ship's posted clearance**, with the driver carrying an English/Korean placard. The vehicle is parked in the **53-bus outdoor parking area** (which fills quickly when both berths are occupied — guides arrive early on dual-ship days). Beginning **May 1, 2025**, **Adora Magic City began semi-homeporting at Gangjeong** with 33 roundtrip voyages planned through end-2025 (5–6 day Shanghai ↔ Jeju ↔ Japan circuits, 60–120 Korean passengers per voyage), increasing the port's profile for both Korean and international cruise lines. The terminal has **better wheelchair accessibility than Jeju Port (north)** per VisitKorea — flat interior, moving walkways, elevators throughout. If guests dock at Gangjeong, the **southern itinerary** is mandatory: the drive north to Seongsan Ilchulbong is **70+ min one-way**, eating the day.

stops.name: Hallasan 1100 Altitude Wetland (Ramsar + UNESCO)
stops.category: Alpine Wetland — Ramsar Site + within Hallasan UNESCO World Heritage
stops.description: **Hallasan 1100 Altitude Wetland (한라산 1100고지 습지)** is a high-elevation alpine wetland system on the Hallasan plateau, located **at exactly 1,100 m above sea level** along **Route 1100 — the highest national highway in Korea** linking Jeju City to Seogwipo Jungmun. The site is a **Ramsar Wetland of International Importance (Site No. 1893, designated 12 October 2009)**, falling within the **Hallasan UNESCO World Heritage Site (2007)**, **Jeju Island UNESCO Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Hallasan National Park (1970)** — making this one of the few sites globally with **four overlapping international/national designations**. The wetland comprises **16+ discontinuous freshwater marshes and pools** spread across a low-gradient plateau section that captures and retains rainwater, forming **small permanent lakes and peat bogs** that support diverse floristic assemblages with significant Jeju endemism. Recorded biodiversity includes **3 threatened bird species and 1 threatened plant species within Korea**, plus mammal, reptile, and insect species endemic to Jeju Island. The site received **900,000 visitors in 2008** (Mt Halla Visitor Centre data). Visitor experience: a **1-km wooden boardwalk loop** with observation decks, interpretive signage, and a small natural-history exhibit at the trailhead. **Autumn foliage (October–November)** and **winter snow scenes (December–February)** are the most photographed seasons; **spring + summer** offer wetland flora and endemic species visibility. **FREE admission**; adjacent **1100 Rest Area (1100 휴게소)** provides indoor restrooms + cafe + small souvenir shop. **Cultural and religious importance** to local communities is documented in the Ramsar listing — historical shaman pilgrimages tied to mountain-water spirits.

stops.name: Daepo Jusangjeolli Cliff
stops.description: **Daepo Jusangjeolli (대포 주상절리, 'Jisatgae')** is a **1 km stretch of hexagonal columnar-jointed basalt cliff** on the Jungmun coast — designated **Natural Monument No. 443 on 2005-01-06**. The columns rise **30–40 m** from the sea, packed in tightly fitted **5- to 7-sided polygons** (mostly hexagons) — a geometry produced when **molten basaltic lava from a Hallasan flank eruption (~250,000–140,000 years ago) reached the ocean and contracted as it cooled**, fracturing perpendicular to the cooling surface. Globally, columnar jointing is best known from **Giant's Causeway (UK)**, **Devils Postpile (US)**, and **Fingal's Cave (Scotland)** — Jusangjeolli is **East Asia's premier example** and the most accessible. The 800 m **boardwalk loop** delivers visitors to a railed cliff edge directly above the columns; **on high-tide days waves break against the columns and throw spray up to 20 m**, occasionally over the rail. Admission: **Adult ₩2,000 / Youth ₩1,000 / FREE under 7 and over 65**. The site is part of the **Jeju Global Geopark (UNESCO designated 2010-10)** — one of **13 official geosites** on the island, alongside Seongsan Ilchulbong, Manjanggul Lava Tube, and Suwolbong. The clifftop deck is **barrier-free** (wheelchair/stroller accessible). Three viewing platforms along the loop give different column angles: the **first platform** shows the columns in profile (best for the hexagonal-geometry shot), the **mid-platform** looks straight down for top-of-column patterns, and the **far platform** captures the 1 km coastal sweep with Hallasan as backdrop on clear days. **Photographic peak window: 14:00–16:00**, when westering sunlight lights the column faces frontally; sunset visits bring silhouette compositions but lose column-detail texture. Hours: **09:00–18:00 (last entry 17:30)**, year-round operation.

stops.whyOnRoute: **Small-group lunch at Olle Maeil Market** — the 75-min window allows guests to **shop the stalls before sitting down** (typical bus-tour practice reverses this), letting the guide help with hallabong/cheonhyehyang tasting, Omegisul liquor sampling, and Jeseong Bakery octopus-bun queueing while purchasing for personal stash. The guide stays with the group through the meal, advising on galchi sashimi vs grilled galchi, recommending which black-pork stall has the day's freshest cut, and translating the family-run vendors who don't speak English. **5-min walk to Cheonjiyeon Falls trailhead** afterward — the small-group format makes this market-to-falls transition feel like an unhurried stroll, not a tour-leader-counted manifest move.

stops.name: Cheonjiyeon Falls (Seogwipo old-town waterfall)
stops.description: **Cheonjiyeon Falls** (천지연 폭포 — literally 'Sky-meeting-Land Falls') is the closer-to-port of Seogwipo's two famous waterfalls and is **NOT to be confused with Cheonjeyeon Falls** (천제연 — the three-tier 'Pond of the Heavenly Emperor' falls in Jungmun, ~15 min west). The single-tier cascade plunges **22 m / 12 m wide into a 20 m-deep emerald pool** at the end of a **gentle 1 km riverside trail** (~15 min one-way, paved and stroller-friendly) that follows a subtropical gorge dense with **rare plants**: **Damselfly Tree** (Natural Monument No. 163), **Crepidomanes makinoi** ferns clinging to the basalt walls, and dozens of endemic species protected as **Natural Monument No. 379** (the falls + surrounding habitat ensemble). The pool below is the protected habitat of the **Mutae eel** (마부태 / 무태 — *Anguilla marmorata*, the marbled eel), itself **Natural Monument No. 27** — a nocturnal species whose movement under nighttime illumination is one of Cheonjiyeon's signature scenes. Admission is **₩2,000 adults / ₩1,000 children** (one of Jeju's most affordable nature stops); operating hours **09:00–22:00 (last entry 21:30)** — the **after-dark illumination program 19:00–22:00** is unique among Jeju waterfalls and turns the gorge walls + falls into an emerald-jade light show, often paired with cruise overnight stays. The trailhead is **a 5-minute walk from Olle Maeil Market**, making the two stops a natural pairing on cruise day-trips originating from Gangjeong port. On clear days, **Munseom Island** (3 km offshore — Korea's first marine-park designation, 1994) is visible from the falls observation deck, and the basalt cliff faces of the gorge expose the same Hallasan-flank lava-flow stratigraphy as Daepo Jusangjeolli.
highlights: **Single-tier cascade 22 m × 12 m into 20 m-deep emerald pool** — Seogwipo's signature waterfall
**NOT Cheonjeyeon (천제연)** — different falls, different gorge, ~15 min west in Jungmun
**Natural Monument No. 379** (falls + endemic-flora habitat ensemble)
**Mutae eel (Natural Monument No. 27)** habitat — nocturnal marbled eel under illumination
**Damselfly Tree (Natural Monument No. 163)** + Crepidomanes makinoi ferns line the gorge
Gentle **1 km paved trail (~15 min)** — stroller/wheelchair friendly
**19:00–22:00 night illumination** — unique among Jeju waterfalls
**5-min walk from Olle Maeil Market** — natural pairing for south-route cruise days

sources: VisitKorea / english.visitkorea.or.kr (Cheonjiyeon Falls dimensions, hours, admission)
Visit Jeju (Natural Monument designations 27, 163, 379; trail length)
Wikipedia (Cheonjiyeon Waterfall — habitat protection, distinction from Cheonjeyeon)
Inside Jeju (hours 09:00–22:00 last entry 21:30; ₩2,000 / ₩1,000 admission)
Trip.com / Tripmoments (night illumination 19:00–22:00; Mutae eel nocturnal habitat)

stops.whyOnRoute: **Cheonjiyeon Falls with small-group depth** — the 75-min window (vs 60 min on bus) covers the full **1-km riverside trail at observation pace**, with attention to the **Damselfly Tree (Natural Monument No. 163)**, **Crepidomanes makinoi** ferns on the basalt walls, and (after dusk on overnight cruise stays) the **Mutae eel (Natural Monument No. 27)** habitat under nighttime illumination. Three Natural Monuments stack at this single site — and small-group narration lets the guide deliver these as a structured **endemic-fauna-ensemble story**, not as bullet-point PA mentions. **Critical**: this is **Cheonjiyeon (천지연)**, NOT **Cheonjeyeon (천제연 — three-tier in Jungmun)** — the names confuse first-time guests, and the small-group format gives the guide explicit time to make this distinction with the gorge stratigraphy in view.

stops.name: Olle Maeil Market (Seogwipo Maeil Olle Market)
stops.description: **Seogwipo Maeil Olle Market** (서귀포 매일올레시장) is **South Jeju's largest traditional market** and the closest food-and-shopping anchor to Gangjeong cruise port (~10 min drive). Established in **the early 1960s**, the market today runs a **620 m covered arcade with ~200 shops and ~140 vendors**, all under a translucent retractable roof that keeps shoppers dry in Jeju's frequent showers. The **'Maeil Olle'** name combines *Maeil* ('every day' — open daily, no fixed closure) with *Olle* (Jeju's famous walking-trail network — Trail Course 6 passes near the market entrance, and Course 7 connects southward to Cheonjiyeon Falls and the Seogwipo waterfront). Signature foods to look for: **Jeseong Bakery's octopus buns** (locally famous, queues form by 10:00); **hallabong + cheonhyehyang citrus** in winter (December–March, the famous Jeju mandarin hybrids); **fresh hairtail (galchi) sashimi**; **black pork skewers**; **Mom-guk** (traditional Jeju seaweed soup); and **Omegisul** (the rice-millet liquor brewed in Seongeup — sold here in tasting bottles). Hours are roughly **07:00–21:00** (vendor-by-vendor variable), and most of the older vendors accept cash only — though tax-refund and card-accepting modern stalls have multiplied since 2018. The market is a **5-minute walk from Cheonjiyeon Falls trailhead**, making Cheonjiyeon + Olle a natural lunch-and-stroll pairing — guides typically schedule lunch (Jeju seafood or black-pork) at one of the market's sit-down stalls before the falls visit, taking advantage of the food density before walking off the meal at the gorge.
highlights: **South Jeju's largest traditional market** — 620 m arcade, ~200 shops, ~140 vendors
Established **early 1960s** — 60+ year continuous market tradition
**Olle Trail Courses 6 & 7** pass near the market entrance
**Jeseong Bakery octopus buns** — local cult favorite, queues by 10:00
**Hallabong + cheonhyehyang citrus** in season (Dec–Mar) — Jeju mandarin hybrids
Hairtail (galchi) sashimi, black pork skewers, Mom-guk (Jeju seaweed soup)
**Omegisul rice-millet liquor** tastings (brewed in Seongeup)
**Translucent retractable roof** — covered arcade, weather-proof shopping
**5-min walk to Cheonjiyeon Falls trailhead** — natural lunch + stroll pairing

sources: VisitKorea / english.visitkorea.or.kr (market dimensions: 620 m, ~200 shops)
Visit Jeju (early-1960s history; signature foods; Olle Trail proximity)
Klook / Mindtrip (vendor count ~140; opening hours)
Soul of Seoul (Olle Maeil Market food guide — Jeseong Bakery octopus buns)
Jeju Olle Foundation (Trail Courses 6 & 7 routing through market area)

stops.whyOnRoute: **Small-group return swing through Olle Maeil Market** — 60-min window (vs 45 min on bus) allows guests to make considered last-minute purchases — premium hallabong gift boxes, Omegisul presentation bottles, the famous **Jeseong Bakery octopus buns** (queues sometimes exceed 30 min — small group can hold a place while others browse), and tax-refund-eligible souvenirs. The **620-m covered arcade with ~140 vendors** is enough food density that the guide can recommend specific stalls for specific items (cheonhyehyang citrus from Vendor X; mom-guk seaweed soup mix from Vendor Y), leveraging local-knowledge depth the bus-tour format can't match. **Most stalls accept Visa/Mastercard** post-2018, important for cruise guests with depleted KRW.

stops.name: Return to Gangjeong Cruise Terminal (Seogwipo — south)
stops.description: Return drive to the **Gangjeong Port Cruise Terminal** in Seogwipo's Gangjeong Village. From the southern circuit (Olle Maeil Market / Cheonjiyeon Falls / downtown Seogwipo area) the drive is short — **~10–15 min via local roads**. From Hallasan 1100 Highland the descent takes ~35 min via Route 1100 (the same highest-altitude national highway you ascended in the morning). Drop-off is timed to deliver guests **at least 60 minutes before your ship's all-aboard time**, matching cruise industry standard buffers. The vehicle pulls into the **53-bus outdoor parking area**, guests disembark at the terminal entrance with luggage assistance from the driver/guide. Inside the terminal: tourist information center (multilingual), convenience store, currency exchange, lounge, tax-refund counter. If guests dock at Gangjeong with same-day evening flights, **Limousine Bus 800 / 800-1** connects from Gangjeong-area to Jeju International Airport via Seogwipo Innovation City (driver must be informed of destination before tap-in). The southern itinerary keeps stops within **30-min radius of the port** — the comfortable 8-hour window is built around this geography. **Note**: Anti-base activists hold daily Catholic mass + 100-bow protests at the main gate (separate from cruise-passenger areas, away from the terminal — cruise guests rarely encounter these activities on the standard arrival/departure path).
highlights: **Drop-off 60+ min before all-aboard time** — cruise industry standard
From Olle Market / Cheonjiyeon area: ~10–15 min drive (port-side circuit)
From Hallasan 1100: ~35 min descent via Route 1100
Driver/guide luggage assistance at terminal entrance
**Limousine Bus 800 / 800-1** connects Gangjeong-area → Jeju Airport via Innovation City
Tourist info center, currency exchange, tax-refund counter on-site

sources: VisitKorea / english.visitkorea.or.kr (Gangjeong facilities + Limousine 800)
Jeju Provincial Government (Limousine 800/800-1 routing via Innovation City)
Korea Cruise Port Sales (terminal-to-airport options)
Cruise industry standard (60-min all-aboard buffer)
VisitJeju.net (southern circuit drive times)

stops.whyOnRoute: **Catalog's shortest cruise return** — Olle Market to Gangjeong port is **~15 minutes** by minicoach. The south route's compact geography (all stops within 30-km radius of port) is the small-group product's signature efficiency: **measurable extra ground time per stop** vs equivalent north-route timing, translating to the deeper observation pacing across all four substantive stops. **60-minute pre-all-aboard buffer** is generous on the south route — guests often use it for pre-sailaway coffee at the terminal lounge or a final photo of the Hallasan silhouette over the harbor breakwater. Guide-to-guest ratio (1:8 to 1:15) vs the bus product (1:25 to 1:40) is the value justification for the premium tier.

matching_metadata.source_of_truth_note: Runtime recommendation reads Supabase match_tours.matching_profile. Cruise products require user_port parameter for route option selection.

port_routing_split.previous_state: Single itineraryStops representing one generic cruise route.
port_routing_split.rationale: Cruise ships call at different Jeju ports depending on route. Jeju Port (north) and Gangjeong Port (south, in Seogwipo) are 60+ km apart, so the day-trip route differs entirely. A single itinerary misrepresented the actual product served at each port.
matching_implications: When user_port is known (cruise booking context), score only the matched variant's poi_tags_in_variant / theme_tags_in_variant.
When user_port is unknown, fall back to matching_profile.poi_tags (union).
Variant-level indoor_ratio_in_variant / weather_sensitivity_in_variant override matching_profile values when a specific port is selected.
downstream_actions: Frontend: add port selector UI (radio or chip) before itinerary renders. Default to 'Select your cruise port' empty state with both variant previews visible.
Supabase: add port_id (text, nullable) column to bookings table; FK to a new cruise_ports reference table with rows for jeju_port, gangjeong_port.
Matching algorithm: cruise products accept user_port as optional parameter. If provided, score variant-specific tags; otherwise score union from matching_profile.
Pricing review: validate whether bus / small group pricing differs by port — Gangjeong route includes Hallasan 1100 which adds drive time and toll vs Jeju Port's east-coast loop.
Existing bookings: backfill port_id where derivable from itinerary or pickup_base; flag rest as null for manual review.
Locale (ko): translate port_label, port_label_short, route_focus, and stop names/descriptions for Korean users.

Variant-specific scoring overrides apply when user_port is known

score_rationale.unesco_fit: Both port variants include UNESCO World Heritage POIs (Seongsan or Hallasan-area).
score_rationale.weather_sensitivity: Cruise schedule is fixed — weather impact is structural.
score_rationale.value_for_money_fit: Small group format: balanced value with smaller-group attention.

staticQuestions.question: 我如何得知我的郵輪停靠哪個港口？
staticQuestions.answer: 您的郵輪預訂確認書上會列出濟州的停靠港口。我們將於預訂時與您確認港口，並據此安排當日行程路線。大多數主要郵輪公司停靠濟州港（北部碼頭）；部分西歸浦地區的靠港班次則使用江汀港。

staticQuestions.question: 我能準時返回船上嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的。兩種路線方案均會在16:30至17:00之間返回郵輪碼頭，並預留緩衝時間應對任何交通延誤。我們會追蹤您的出航時間，並視情況調整行程節奏。

staticQuestions.question: 如果我的郵輪靠港地點更改了怎麼辦？
staticQuestions.answer: 請儘快通知我們——我們將切換至對應的路線方案。港口路線規劃正是此行程設有兩種方案而非一種的主要原因。

staticQuestions.question: 這對首次到訪濟州的旅客來說是個好選擇嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的——它在均衡的一天行程中涵蓋了該地區最具特色的景點。

staticQuestions.question: 步行難度高嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 步行難度適中，部分路段較為費體力。

staticQuestions.question: 此行程適合家庭參加嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的——大多數家庭都能輕鬆參與。

staticQuestions.question: 年長者可以輕鬆參加嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 適合行動能力良好的年長者；對行動不便的旅客則較不理想。

staticQuestions.question: 建議的最小參加年齡是多少？
staticQuestions.answer: 最適合6歲及以上的兒童——能適應全天行程節奏且符合上述步行要求的孩子。

staticQuestions.question: 行程可以在雨天進行嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 部分景點位於露天環境；天氣非常潮濕時，可能會縮短或替換部分停留景點。

staticQuestions.question: 午餐是否包含在內？
staticQuestions.answer: 行程路線中已安排午餐休息時間。用餐本身是否包含在內取決於所訂費率——請查看包含項目清單。

staticQuestions.question: 我在哪裡上車？
staticQuestions.answer: 從您的飯店大廳或預訂區域的指定接送地點出發。確切的接送時間將於前一晚確認。

itineraryStops.description: 聯合國教科文組織世界遺產凝灰岩錐體——約5,000年前由水下噴發形成的水火山口，從海面升起約180公尺，擁有近乎完美的火山口邊緣。可選擇沿石階（約600階）攀爬約25分鐘至火山口邊緣觀景台；不打算攀登者可改走基地沿海步道。2007年以「濟州火山島與熔岩管」之名列入聯合國教科文組織世界遺產名錄。
highlights: 聯合國教科文組織世界遺產火山口
可選擇登頂至火山口邊緣
基地沿海觀景點

visitBasics.walking: 中等難度——需持續攀爬石階20至30分鐘（約600階）；每隔數分鐘設有長椅供休息

smartNotes.tip: 全程石階但維護良好——穿著便服即可應付；設有休息站，適合家庭出遊；日出時段深受歡迎，黎明前即有人潮聚集
smartNotes.photo: 晴天時可在山頂眺望西側的漢拏山與北側的牛島；下山途中可欣賞海女黑沙灘景色
smartNotes.facilities: 咖啡廳（距離1.3公里的Fritz Coffee擁有最佳的山峰外觀視野）；可從城山港（步行約1.5公里／15分鐘）搭乘渡輪前往牛島

itineraryStops.name: 涉地可支
itineraryStops.description: 位於濟州島東端的火山海岸岬角，因其小型錐狀地形而得名。沿海步道平緩易行，全長約1.5公里，可回望城山日出峰、挾才燈塔，以及「All-In之家」（為2003年韓劇《All In》搭建的小教堂場景，現已改設為小型博物館）。免費入場；春季時油菜花盛開，鋪滿整片岬角。

itineraryStops.name: 城邑民俗村
itineraryStops.description: 這座村莊已有600年連續居住歷史，建於1423年，為朝鮮時代濟州三大行政區之一「旌義縣」的行政中心。1984年被指定為國家民俗文化遺產。旌義縣城牆內保存有居民仍在使用的茅草屋頂民居、旌義鄉校儒學學堂（濟州有形文化財第5號）、一官軒官廳、數百年古老的榉樹與朴樹、石爺爺（돌하르방）石像，以及傳統「통시」（豬圈與廁所合一的設施）。本村落並非聯合國教科文組織世界遺產——僅具韓國國家民俗遺產指定資格。
highlights: 傳統茅草屋頂民居

staticQuestions.question: 我如何知道我的郵輪停靠哪個港口？
staticQuestions.answer: 您的郵輪預訂確認書上會列明濟州靠港地點。我們在預訂時與您確認港口，並據此規劃當日路線。大多數主要郵輪公司靠泊濟州港（北方碼頭）；部分西歸浦區域的靠港則使用江汀港。

staticQuestions.question: 我能準時返回船上嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的。兩個行程版本均會在16:30至17:00之間返回郵輪碼頭，並預留應對交通延誤的緩衝時間。我們會追蹤您的啟航時間，並在情況有變時調整行程節奏。

staticQuestions.question: 如果我的郵輪靠港地點有變怎麼辦？
staticQuestions.answer: 請您一知道就通知我們——我們會隨即切換相應的路線版本。港口路線規劃正是本行程設有兩個版本的原因所在。

staticQuestions.question: 這個行程適合初次造訪濟州的遊客嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的——它在均衡的一日行程中涵蓋了該地區最具特色的景點。

staticQuestions.question: 步行難度高嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 步行難度適中，部分路段較為活潑積極。

staticQuestions.question: 這個行程適合家庭參加嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的——大多數家庭均可輕鬆參與。

staticQuestions.question: 年長者可以舒適地參加嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 活動能力良好的年長者適合參加；行動不便的旅客則較不理想。

staticQuestions.question: 建議的最低年齡是多少？
staticQuestions.answer: 最適合6歲或以上的兒童——能適應全天行程節奏及上述步行要求的孩童皆可參加。

staticQuestions.question: 雨天也能進行此行程嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 部分景點為露天環境；遇上大雨天氣，行程可能縮短或更換景點。

staticQuestions.question: 午餐包含在內嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 路線中已安排午餐休息時間。餐點本身是否包含於費用內，視預訂方案而定——請查閱包含項目清單。

staticQuestions.question: 我在哪裡上車？
staticQuestions.answer: 在您的飯店大廳或預訂區域的指定接送地點。確切的接送時間將於前一天晚上確認。

v17_schema_unification.description: Phase 1: Standardized matching_profile scores to the current 0-1 float scale used by match_tours.matching_profile.

v17_batch6_enhancement.description: Batch 6 deep enhancement — port-aware cruise shore excursion tour. Both variants (Jeju Port north + Gangjeong Port south) enhanced with 1,000+ char descriptions per stop; KB-backed _poi_meta on all attraction stops; matching_profile recalibrated to verified-fact 0-1 float scores; variant-level legacy 0-5 / 0-100 scale fields ALSO normalized to 0-1 float (Phase 1 unification completion for variant scope). Time-weighted indoor_ratio computed per variant per Fix 1 methodology.

_score_audit._methodology: 0-1 float scale per Phase 1 standard. Variant-level legacy 0-5/0-100 scale fields ALSO normalized to 0-1 float in this batch (previously the schema unification stopped at top-level matching_profile). Time-weighted indoor_ratio per Fix 1 — computed PER VARIANT then aggregated. Top-level matching_profile uses cross-variant averages weighted toward Gangjeong (the more common 2025+ docking).

unesco_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED HIGH — both variants hit major UNESCO sites. Variant 0 (Jeju Port): Seongsan Ilchulbong = TRIPLE UNESCO (World Natural Heritage 2007 + Biosphere Reserve + Global Geopark) + Haenyeo ICH demos. Variant 1 (Gangjeong): Hallasan UNESCO World Heritage + Biosphere Reserve + Jusangjeolli Global Geopark. Both variants reliably deliver UNESCO content. 0.85 reflects this consistent delivery. Note: Seongeup is NOT UNESCO (national folklore only). Olle Market and Cheonjiyeon are not UNESCO. Score reflects weighted average of UNESCO density in both variants.

scenic_level.reasoning: VERIFIED — Variant 0: volcanic crater + coastal cape + folk village. Variant 1: alpine wetland + columnar basalt + waterfall + market. Both deliver catalog-leading scenic diversity. 0.95 calibrated.

photo_level.reasoning: Verified — UNESCO crater, columnar basalt, subtropical falls, thatched-roof village, market food shots. Highly photogenic but not the absolute top (no flower festivals). 0.95 calibrated.

walking_level.reasoning: VERIFIED — Variant 0: Seongsan optional summit climb (300-500 stairs is the limit case) + Seongeup village walking; Variant 1: Hallasan boardwalk (flat) + Jusangjeolli flat + Cheonjiyeon flat. Variant 1 is materially flatter than Variant 0. Aggregate 0.55 reflects mostly easy walking with the Seongsan summit as the harder option (always optional).

indoor_ratio.reasoning: Time-weighted (Fix 1) — Variant 0 substantive 0.12, Variant 1 substantive 0.18. Aggregate cross-variant ~0.15-0.20. Catalog value 0.20 (slight upward adjustment to account for cruise-day bus-shelter time + lunch indoor).

weather_sensitivity.reasoning: UPGRADED — cruise day has hard time constraint + ship cannot wait + the route is mostly outdoor. Heavy rain at Hallasan 1100 (road closure risk) or storm at Seongsan summit climb meaningfully compromises the day. 0.85 reflects this elevated cruise-context weather risk vs ordinary 0.80 day tour.

rain_fit.reasoning: Variant 1 is meaningfully more rain-friendly than Variant 0 (Olle Market covered + Hallasan boardwalk under tree canopy + Cheonjiyeon's subtropical forest provides some shelter). Variant 0's Seongsan climb is exposed. Aggregate 0.45 — slightly improved over old 0.40 calibration.

iconic_landmark_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED — verified: Variant 0 has Seongsan Ilchulbong (Jeju's single most-iconic landmark, Triple UNESCO). Variant 1 has Hallasan UNESCO + Jusangjeolli Natural Monument. Both variants deliver catalog-leading iconic content. 0.95 — among the highest in the catalog.

first_time_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED — cruise day for first-time Jeju visitors is the ARCHETYPE use case for this product. Both variants deliver the iconic Jeju vista appropriate to the docking port. 0.95 — near-maximum first-time fit.

same_day_flight_fit.reasoning: Cruise tour ends 16:30-17:30 at port; not at airport. Bus #100 from Jeju Port to airport is direct (~30 min). For the Gangjeong variant, airport transfer is ~50 min via Limousine 800. Both variants support same-day onward travel for guests leaving the cruise mid-itinerary, but it's tighter from Gangjeong. Aggregate 0.95.

small_group_fit_smallgroup.reasoning: Per-tour: small_group tour = 1.0 (small group format defines this product); bus tour = 0.2 (large coach format is the OTHER product). Format fixed by tour SKU.

bus_fit_smallgroup.reasoning: Per-tour: small_group tour bus_fit = 0.2; bus tour bus_fit = 1.0.

private_fit.reasoning: Neither tour is private — both are group products (small group OR large coach). 0.15.

premium_fit_smallgroup.reasoning: Small group cruise tour reads premium — fewer guests, more guide attention, easier pace. Bus tour reads budget — economies of scale, larger group, less flexibility. Calibrated per tour.

value_for_money_fit_smallgroup.reasoning: Bus tour delivers stronger value (lower per-person cost for same itinerary). Small group is mid-value (premium-priced but delivers premium experience). Calibrated per tour.

young_kids_fit.reasoning: Cruise day with kids works in Variant 1 (Gangjeong) better — Olle Market food + flat boardwalk + waterfall walk all kid-friendly. Variant 0's Seongsan summit climb is the limit case for younger kids. 0.55 aggregate.

family_fit.reasoning: Verified — UNESCO sights + market food + folk village hit intergenerational interests. Aggregate 0.65.

senior_fit.reasoning: Variant 1 is materially more senior-friendly (boardwalk + flat market + flat falls path). Variant 0's Seongsan summit is the limit. Aggregate 0.65 — moderate senior fit.

mobility_friendly_fit.reasoning: Variant 1 stronger (Gangjeong terminal reportedly has better wheelchair accessibility than Jeju Port; Hallasan boardwalk stroller-friendly; Cheonjiyeon flat path; covered Olle Market). Variant 0's Seongsan climb is the limit. 0.55 aggregate.

stroller_fit.reasoning: Variant 1 fully stroller-accessible. Variant 0's Seongsan summit stairs not accessible. Aggregate 0.50.

nature_fit.reasoning: Verified — UNESCO volcano + alpine wetland + columnar basalt + subtropical waterfall. 0.90.

shopping_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED — Variant 1's Olle Market is a strong shopping anchor (souvenirs + foodie items). Variant 0 lighter on shopping. 0.55 aggregate.

food_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED — Variant 1's Olle Market plus Seogwipo lunch cluster deliver strong food anchor. Variant 0 lunch only. 0.65 aggregate. Black pork + Hallabong + abalone all featured.

local_culture_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED — Variant 0's Seongeup Folk Village (600-year living town) is high-cultural-density. Variant 1's Olle Market is high-local-life-density. Aggregate 0.70.

storytelling_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED — cruise day tours are storytelling-heavy (one-shot first-time guests, guide narrates everything). Both variants have rich histories: Seongsan formation + haenyeo tradition + Joseon-era folk village (Variant 0); Hallasan UNESCO + columnar basalt geology + 1,500-year tangerine heritage + Mutae eel mythology + Gangjeong civilian-military history (Variant 1). 0.85.

cafe_fit.reasoning: Modest cafe content — Olle Market has cafe stalls; Hallasan 1100 rest area cafe; otherwise no dedicated cafe stops. 0.40 calibrated.

comfort_level.reasoning: Cruise day infrastructure is comfort-optimized — paved/boardwalk trails, climate-controlled vehicles, restaurant lunch, terminal facilities. 0.75 aggregate.

duration_hours.reasoning: Confirmed 8h duration for both variants — within cruise day buffer.

min_recommended_age.reasoning: Confirmed 6+ — both variants accessible to school-age children.
